plan_linkage.plan_title: Implementation Plan - SQL Server Residue Eradication

summary.plan_title: Implementation Plan - SQL Server Residue Eradication

rules.description: Warn when advanced controls are visible before the primary objective is established.
rules.name: Advanced Options Exposed Too Early

steps: Move advanced settings behind progressive disclosure.

rules.description: Raw artifacts are treated as the system of record instead of referenced evidence attached to persisted records.
rules.name: Architecture Artifact Payload As Operational Truth

steps: Persist control-plane state in normalized tables.
Reference artifacts through manifests, hashes, and evidence links.
Do not let artifact payloads become workflow truth.

rules.description: Code crosses bounded domains or mixes unrelated business contexts in the same implementation surface.
rules.name: Architecture Domain Context Bleed

steps: Split the module at the domain boundary.
Move policy decisions into the governance layer.
Keep workflow-specific logic isolated from unrelated domains.

rules.description: A Python file dynamically loads modules from a sibling repository at runtime by manipulating sys.path. This pattern hard-codes a local filesystem path into the execution model, making the code non-portable across environments, Docker containers, and deployments that do not co-locate both repos.
rules.name: Architecture External Repo Dependency

steps: Publish the external repo as an installable package and declare it as a dependency.
Expose the capability as an HTTP API or subprocess contract with a documented interface.
If co-location is unavoidable short-term, document the external path as an explicit runtime dependency with a health check on startup.

signals: sys.path.insert or sys.path.append with external path
importlib.import_module in same file as sys.path manipulation
hardcoded absolute path to sibling repository

rules.description: The frontend or local client state is treated as the owner of workflow status or approval truth.
rules.name: Architecture Frontend-Owned Workflow Truth

steps: Persist workflow truth in SQL first.
Project read models to the UI.
Use events only as invalidation hints for refetching authoritative state.

rules.description: A single class owns too many behaviors, dependencies, or policy decisions.
rules.name: Architecture God Class

steps: Split the class into narrower services.
Introduce explicit ports for persistence and review decisions.
Keep classes aligned to one bounded concern.

rules.description: A single file concentrates too many responsibilities, branches, or integration concerns to remain governable.
rules.name: Architecture God File

steps: Extract cohesive services by responsibility.
Move orchestration into thin coordination layers.
Keep persistence, governance, and API concerns in separate modules.

rules.description: Block discovery code that builds pattern inventory or architecture posture from markdown-first synthesis instead of governed evidence surfaces.
rules.name: Markdown-First Pattern Discovery

steps: Resolve patterns from SQL-backed evidence surfaces first.
Use markdown only for review or projection output.

rules.description: Block code that assembles markdown artifacts as a source of truth when the governed source of truth is SQL-backed state.
rules.name: Markdown Hand-Stitching Instead Of SQL SoT

steps: Persist the authoritative state in SQL first.
Treat markdown as a projection or export only.

rules.description: Block capability or orchestration code that mutates files directly instead of routing through a governed wrapper or workflow-owned mutation surface.
rules.name: One-Off Mutation Path Outside Wrapper

steps: Move the mutation behind a governed wrapper or workflow-bound tool.
Do not introduce one-off file mutation paths in skill runtime code.

rules.description: Approval or policy logic is implemented outside the governance layer or persisted review records.
rules.name: Architecture Policy Outside Governance

steps: Move policy evaluation into governance services.
Persist reviewer decisions before projecting state.
Keep UI surfaces as projections of backend governance truth.

signals: review logic in UI or scripts

rules.description: Transient prompt or context assembly is treated as durable system memory.
rules.name: Architecture Prompt Context As Durable State

steps: Persist durable state explicitly in SQL.
Treat prompts as transient execution context.
Recover workflows from persisted records, not chat context.

rules.description: Durable workflow or governance truth is duplicated into unmanaged JSON blobs that drift from SQL.
rules.name: Architecture SQL Truth Duplicated In JSON

steps: Keep SQL as the durable control plane.
Store large artifacts as referenced manifests, not operational truth.
Limit JSON blobs to evidence or transport payloads.

rules.description: The implementation exposes too many endpoints, handlers, or orchestration branches for the intended workflow shape.
rules.name: Architecture Surface Area Explosion

steps: Reduce the public surface to the operator-critical lanes.
Collapse duplicative entry points behind shared contracts.
Prefer projections over bespoke one-off endpoints.

rules.description: Modules depend directly on too many concrete collaborators or bleed across layer boundaries.
rules.name: Architecture Tight Coupling

steps: Introduce explicit service boundaries.
Depend on narrow contracts instead of concrete implementations.
Separate orchestration from infrastructure concerns.

rules.description: Block dashboard-first experiences for first-time completion or onboarding flows.
rules.name: Dashboard Used For First-Time Setup

steps: Switch to a guided or review-first workflow shell.

rules.description: A workflow or runtime authority surface claims or implies a deployment/database posture that differs from the governed operating reality of Replit plus Neon PostgreSQL.
rules.name: Deployment Truth Mismatch

steps: Update workflow and runtime authority surfaces to name Replit as the deployment target and Neon PostgreSQL as the database authority.
Do not leave mixed Azure/Replit or Azure SQL/PostgreSQL language in active deployment and validation paths.

signals: active workflow missing Neon or PostgreSQL references
deployment metadata disagrees with runtime authority record

rules.description: Warn when a low-expertise audience is routed into a dense technical layout.
rules.name: High Density Layout For Low Expertise

steps: Use a lower-density shell with clearer chunking.

rules.description: Block medium or high-risk irreversible actions that do not provide preview-before-commit sequencing.
rules.name: Irreversible Action Without Preview

steps: Require preview-before-commit sequencing.

rules.description: Block high-cost actions when the user cannot revise, undo, or backtrack safely.
rules.name: No Visible Recovery Path

steps: Add backtrack, undo, or clear revision affordances before approval.

rules.description: Active codebase-shaping workflows treat SQL-backed shaping as optional and skip execution when PostgreSQL-backed shaping should fail closed.
rules.name: Optional Codebase Shaping

steps: Fail closed when PostgreSQL shaping configuration is missing on operational runs.
Keep inventory sync, symbol sync, export, and verification in the active shaping pipeline.
Do not treat SQL-backed code shaping as optional operational behavior.

signals: codebase shape workflow exits 0 on missing PG config
active sync omits required inventory/symbol/export/verify chain

rules.description: Active runtime, workflow, or validation surfaces still reference Azure deployment or SQL Server infrastructure after the Replit and Neon authority transition.
rules.name: Stale Runtime Infrastructure Reference

steps: Remove Azure and SQL Server deployment references from active runtime and CI/CD surfaces.
Keep any remaining Azure references explicitly archived or legacy-only.
Prefer Replit deployment language and Neon PostgreSQL configuration in active validation paths.

signals: azure deployment token in active workflow
SQL Server / ODBC runtime references in active deployment path

rules.description: Warn when the screen presents several competing top-level actions with equal visual weight.
rules.name: Too Many Equal Weight Actions

steps: Reduce visible top-level actions to one primary path.

workflow_candidates.description: Composes svg-editor topology and normalization scripts into a reusable visual-asset workflow candidate.
workflow_candidates.goal: Normalize, scale, and enrich SVG topology assets before downstream rendering.
workflow_candidates.name: normalize_svg_topology_assets

workflow_candidates.description: Composes ai-engine export and reporting runners into a reusable operational reporting candidate.
workflow_candidates.goal: Run review, reporting, and snapshot export loops from ai-engine operational scripts.
workflow_candidates.name: export_ai_engine_operational_reports

workflow_candidates.description: Packages WealthBuilder RAG scripts into a candidate financial retrieval workflow.
workflow_candidates.goal: Generate embeddings and example financial RAG outputs for the WealthBuilder knowledge stack.
workflow_candidates.name: build_financial_rag_workspace

workflow_candidates.description: Composes StockScoutPython scripts into a reusable holdings intelligence workflow candidate.
workflow_candidates.goal: Download, analyze, and export holdings intelligence artifacts for financial research.
workflow_candidates.name: produce_financial_holdings_intelligence

workflow_candidates.description: Packages RenderX mono-repo shell sync scripts into a catalog synchronization workflow candidate.
workflow_candidates.goal: Synchronize plugin, sequence, component, and interaction catalogs for the RenderX shell.
workflow_candidates.name: sync_renderx_shell_catalogs

workflow_candidates.description: Composes Loga render and compiler scripts into a reusable workflow compilation candidate.
workflow_candidates.goal: Render, validate, and compile structured workflow assets through the Loga pipeline.
workflow_candidates.name: run_loga_compiler_pipeline

workflow_candidates.description: Packages Appture compiler scripts into a candidate surface-compilation workflow.
workflow_candidates.goal: Parse sketches, compile routes, and update tracker artifacts for Appture surfaces.
workflow_candidates.name: compile_appture_surface_assets

workflow_candidates.description: Composes AC-alignment scripts into a reusable test-alignment workflow candidate.
workflow_candidates.goal: Map, score, tag, and remediate acceptance-criteria coverage across tests.
workflow_candidates.name: align_acceptance_criteria_to_tests

workflow_candidates.description: Chains OgraphX analysis and generator scripts into a graph intelligence workflow candidate.
workflow_candidates.goal: Analyze code graphs and generate sequence and diagram artifacts from OgraphX tooling.
workflow_candidates.name: analyze_and_generate_ographx_artifacts

workflow_candidates.description: Composes renderx digital-asset generator scripts into a reusable storyboard workflow candidate.
workflow_candidates.goal: Generate scene, graph, and presentation artifacts for digital asset storyboards.
workflow_candidates.name: generate_digital_asset_storyboards

workflow_candidates.description: Aggregates verification scripts into a reusable validation workflow candidate.
workflow_candidates.goal: Run repository-specific validation scripts after generation workflows.
workflow_candidates.name: validate_generated_artifacts

workflow_candidates.description: Composes media-processing scripts into a candidate editorial workflow.
workflow_candidates.goal: Transform speech or transcript assets into article-ready outputs.
workflow_candidates.name: convert_speech_to_article_assets

workflow_candidates.description: Composes resume and job-related scripts into a reusable application workflow candidate.
workflow_candidates.goal: Prepare tailored resume artifacts for a specific job posting.
workflow_candidates.name: prepare_job_application_packet
